pattern: Reasoning backward from the target, halving when it is even and incrementing when it is odd, turns the doubling/decrement search into a greedy linear walk down to the start value.
hints: Doubling forward branches explosively, but running the operations in reverse from the target is deterministic.
In reverse, doubling becomes halving (only valid on an even number) and subtracting one becomes adding one.
While target > startValue: if target is even halve it, else add one; count each step. Once target <= startValue, add (startValue - target) more decrements.

edge_inputs.description: already at target needs no operations
target far above start needs many doublings

stress.description: small start and target so the BFS oracle can cross-check the greedy
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s = rng.randint(1, 5000)
    t = rng.randint(1, 50000)
    return [s, t]
stress.note: target under the BFS threshold so both oracle paths agree

stress.description: large random start and target across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    t = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    return [s, t]
stress.note: exercises the greedy on the full constraint range
